snippets.title: Create, read, update, delete
snippets.code:
prices = {"gpt-4o": 0.005, "gpt-4o-mini": 0.00015}

print(prices["gpt-4o"])            # 0.005       - O(1)
print("gpt-3.5" in prices)         # False        - O(1) membership

prices["gpt-4.1"] = 0.0025          # insert       - O(1)
prices["gpt-4o-mini"] = 0.00016     # update       - O(1)
del prices["gpt-4o"]                # delete       - O(1)

for name, p in prices.items():      # ordered iteration (insertion order)
    print(name, p)
snippets.explanation: All core operations are O(1) average; iteration follows insertion order.

snippets.title: Safe defaults
snippets.explanation: `get` returns a default instead of raising; perfect for caches and config reads.

snippets.title: Grouping with defaultdict
snippets.explanation: defaultdict(list) auto-creates an empty list on first access, removing the `if k not in d` boilerplate.

snippets.title: Counting frequencies
snippets.code:
from collections import Counter

freq = Counter("the cat the mat the cat".split())
print(freq.most_common(2))   # [('the', 3), ('cat', 2)]
snippets.explanation: Counter is a dict subclass specialized for tallies — ideal for token/word frequency.

snippets.title: Hashable keys
snippets.code:
ok = {("chat", "v1"): 1.0}    # tuple of hashables -> OK

bad = {[1, 2]: "x"}           # TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'
bad = {{1, 2}: "x"}           # TypeError: unhashable type: 'set'
snippets.explanation: Keys must be hashable. Lists/sets/dicts can't be keys because they're mutable.
